name: Vidriales Valley Roman Sites Loop
name_original: Brime de Urz – vuelta de la Sierra de Carpurias y el Castillo de los condes de Benavente
description: This moderate 33.7-mile road cycling route explores the Sierra de Carpurias and historical sites like the Castillo de la Mota near Benavente
region_intro: Road cycling around Quintanilla De Urz offers diverse landscapes within the Zamora province of Spain. The region is characterized by uneven terrain, irrigated by the Almucera stream, and features a mix of fields and picturesque countryside. Road cycling routes here often traverse historic paths, including sections of the Vía de la Plata, providing a blend of cultural immersion and scenic beauty. The area's tranquil roads wind through a preserved rural environment, ideal for observing indigenous flora and fauna. Best road cycling routes around Quintanilla De Urz The most popular road cycling route is Vidriales Valley Roman Sites Loop, a 33.7 miles (54.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores historical sites within the Vidriales Valley. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Tera and Vidriales Valleys Loop, a moderate 55.4 miles (89.1 km) path. This route offers extensive views across both the Tera and Vidriales Valleys. Local road cyclists also love the Granucillo Castle – Arrabalde Church loop from Pozuelo de Vidriales, a 26.7 miles (43.0 km) trail leading through historic villages and past architectural landmarks, often completed in about 1 hour 56 minutes. Road cycling around Quintanilla De Urz is defined by historic roads, tranquil rural landscapes, and views of traditional architecture. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels, with 4 easy and 5 moderate routes available. The routes in Quintanilla De Urz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 40 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Quintanilla De Urz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Quintanilla De Urz?
A: There are 9 road cycling routes available around Quintanilla De Urz. These routes offer a mix of easy and moderate options, with 4 easy and 5 moderate trails to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Quintanilla De Urz?
A: Road cycling in Quintanilla De Urz features diverse and often uneven terrain, characteristic of the Zamora province. You'll encounter picturesque countryside, fields, and winding paths irrigated by the Almucera stream. The routes often traverse historic roads, providing a blend of scenic beauty and cultural immersion.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest to see along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and offers several landmarks. You can cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Vizana&lt;/a&gt;, part of the ancient Vía de la Plata. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marta de Tera&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique equinox illumination, is also accessible. Further afield, the imposing remains of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel&lt;/a&gt; offer a rewarding viewpoint over the Órbigo valley.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Quintanilla De Urz?
A: The region's natural environment, with its abundant flora and fauna, makes it ideal for outdoor activities. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, offering mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes, perfect for enjoying the tranquil roads and observing indigenous wildlife.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023238917/vidriales-valley-roman-sites-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidriales Valley Roman Sites Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025533811/tera-and-vidriales-valleys-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tera and Vidriales Valleys Loop&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that allow you to start and end in the same location.

Q: What are some options for easy road cycling routes for beginners?
A: For those seeking an easier ride, there are 4 easy routes available. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granucillo Castle – Arrabalde Church loop from Pozuelo de Vidriales&lt;/a&gt;, which is 26.7 miles (43.0 km) and leads through historic villages.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the local villages and traditional architecture?
A: Absolutely. The routes often wind through charming villages, allowing you to experience the authentic rural character of the region. Quintanilla de Urz itself features traditional architecture with stone houses and red tile roofs, and you can visit the Parish Church of San Pelayo and the 17th-century Hermitage of San Isidro.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Quintanilla De Urz?
A: The road cycling routes in Quintanilla De Urz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often appreciate the tranquil rural landscapes, the blend of historical sites, and the well-preserved natural environment.

Q: Are there any routes that offer extensive views of the valleys?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025533811/tera-and-vidriales-valleys-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tera and Vidriales Valleys Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 55.4 miles (89.1 km) route specifically designed to offer extensive views across both the Tera and Vidriales Valleys, showcasing the region's natural beauty.

Q: Is there a route that passes by the Church of Santa Marta de Tera?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camarzana de Tera – Church of Santa Marta de Tera loop from Gasolinera Monumento al Camionero&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 48.1 km route that specifically includes the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marta de Tera&lt;/a&gt;, located on the Camino Sanabrés.

Q: Are there any routes that include a bridge over the Tera River?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marta de Tera – Tera Bridge loop from Gasolinera Monumento al Camionero&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 56.8 km route that will take you past the Tera Bridge.
tips: A small town in the northeast of Zamora near the Sierra de Carpurias and the Eria river.

A small town north of the province of Zamora. If you have time for a detour and the right set of wheels, you should head up and visit the Sierra de las Labradas, an archaeological site above the city. This town has important archaeological remains, including one from the Neolithic, located 1 kilometer from the town, at the top of the Sierra de Capurias, is the Castro de Las Labradas, a large fortified town from the Asturian era of the Age of Iron.

An archaeological site of great importance, the Roman Camp of Petavonium is a vestige of the Roman Empire in the Iberian Peninsula. The camp was a fortified outpost protecting trade routes in the area.

Famous for its wines, cheeses and sausages. Santibáñez de Vidriales is a small town located in the north of the province of Zamora. Surrounded by a great wealth of both plants and a diversity of animals, such as deer, wild boar, rabbits and a wide variety of birds.

Ruins of a palace and castle built in the 15th century by members of the Escobar family, who were the lords of the town. Of the Palace, now in a state of abandonment, the outer masonry walls have been preserved. The castle is visible from the road.

faqs.question: Where can I park when cycling this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for cyclists at the Castle of the Counts of Benavente isn't detailed, Benavente, being a historic town, offers various parking options. It's advisable to look for public parking areas within the town, especially near the Morales de Rey or the castle itself, which is also known as Castillo de la Mota.

faqs.question: Is this racebike tour dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebikes and primarily follows roads, which are generally not ideal for dogs, especially off-leash. While dogs are often allowed in public outdoor spaces, the nature of a racebike tour means it's not typically considered dog-friendly for accompanying pets. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can be safely transported in a trailer or carrier suitable for cycling.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Brime de Urz - tour of the Sierra de Carpurias and the Castle of the Counts of Benavente?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route would generally be during spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October). During these months, the weather in the Zamora province of Castile and León is typically milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable exploration of both the natural landscapes and historical sites.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits to cycle this racebike route in the Zamora province of Castile and León, Spain. Public roads and established cycling paths are generally open for recreational use.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: This racebike tour offers varied terrain. While the main roads connecting towns like Brime de Urz and Benavente are generally paved, be prepared for potentially unpaved or gravel sections, especially if you venture deeper into the Sierra de Carpurias. Some routes in the region can have segments that are unpaved and challenging for a racebike, so it's good to be aware of mixed road conditions.

faqs.question: How challenging is this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: This tour is rated as moderate. While the overall elevation gain is around 293 meters, the Sierra de Carpurias includes significant climbs to points like Pico Carpurias. Routes in the Benavente area can involve substantial elevation gains, requiring good fitness. Be prepared for gradients typical of mountain terrain within the Sierra.

faqs.question: What historical sites and natural landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in both history and natural beauty. You'll pass through the historic town of Benavente, home to the prominent Castillo de la Mota (Castle of the Counts of Benavente). Other highlights include Castro de Las Labradas and Petavonium Roman Camp Ruins. The Sierra de Carpurias offers magnificent natural landscapes with pine forests, holm oaks, and panoramic views from higher points.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay overnight near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Benavente and smaller villages like Morales de Rey and Santibáñez de Vidriales Village Highlights offer options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. Benavente, as a larger town, will have the most extensive selection of services for cyclists looking to refuel or stay overnight.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for unpaved sections, it's recommended to bring a well-maintained racebike with durable tires, possibly with a slightly wider profile if you anticipate gravel. Essential gear includes a helmet, water bottles, nutrition, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), appropriate cycling apparel for the weather, and navigation tools. Layers are advisable due to potential elevation changes.

faqs.question: Are there any unique local stories or features along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the region has its share of folklore. An interesting local legend connects 'La Cueva del Lobo' (The Wolf's Cave) in the Sierra de Verdenosa (part of Carpurias) to the Castillo de la Mota de Benavente, adding a touch of mystery and history to your journey through these landscapes.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several local paths. You may find yourself on sections of the Camino de los Navales, Camino de Valdeabuyacos / Camino C-2, Camino Zamorano, and various other local roads and tracks such as Calle Velázquez and Camino del Cementerio.
